Paragraph

We already discussed the necessary part. For the sufficient part, suppose that \(f\) is absolutely continuous on \([a, b]\text{.}\) Then, according to our discussion above, \(f'(x)\) exists \(a.e.\) on \([a, b]\) and is an element in \(L^{1}[a, b]\text{.}\) Set \(F(x)=\int_{a}^{x} f'(t)\, dt\text{.}\) Then, according to Theorem 4.3, \(F(x)\) is is absolutely continuous on \([a, b]\) and \(F'(x)=f'(x)\) \(a.e.\) on \([a, b]\text{.}\)
in-context
